title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: Posterior predictive checking (PPC) principled, simulation-based technique assessing Bayesian model fit. Rather relying solely likelihood-based information criteria, PPC asks direct question: model correct, generate data look like data actually observed?

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: Given Bayesian model likelihood p(y∣θ)p(y \mid \theta) prior p(θ)p(\theta), inference yields posterior p(θ∣y)∝p(y∣θ)p(θ)p(\theta \mid y) \propto p(y \mid \theta)\, p(\theta). posterior predictive distribution future — hypothetically replicated — outcome yrepy^{rep} p(yrep∣y)=∫p(yrep∣θ)p(θ∣y)dθ. p(y^{rep} \mid y)   = \int p(y^{rep} \mid \theta)\, p(\theta \mid y)\, d\theta. practice integral approximated Monte Carlo averaging SS posterior draws θ(1),…,θ(S)\theta^{(1)}, \ldots, \theta^{(S)}: p(yrep∣y)≈1S∑s=1Sp(yrep∣θ(s)). p(y^{rep} \mid y)   \approx \frac{1}{S} \sum_{s=1}^{S} p\!\left(y^{rep} \mid \theta^{(s)}\right). draw yrep(s)y^{rep(s)} complete replicated dataset size yy. collection {yrep(s)}s=1S\{y^{rep(s)}\}_{s=1}^{S} forms posterior predictive ensemble used visual numerical diagnostics.

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: scalar test statistic T(⋅)T(\cdot) summarises property dataset (e.g. mean, standard deviation, tail quantile). Bayesian pp-value TT pB=Pr(T(yrep)≥T(y)∣y)≈1S∑s=1S𝟏[T(yrep(s))≥T(y)]. p_B = \Pr\!\left(T(y^{rep}) \geq T(y) \mid y\right)     \approx \frac{1}{S}\sum_{s=1}^{S}             \mathbf{1}\!\left[T(y^{rep(s)}) \geq T(y)\right]. value pB≈0.5p_B \approx 0.5 indicates model well-calibrated respect TT. Extreme values (pB<0.05p_B < 0.05 pB>0.95p_B > 0.95) flag potential misfit.

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: Install predictCheckR GitHub (published) load source directory development:
code: library(predictCheckR) library(ggplot2)

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: predictCheckR ships example_data, small simulated regression dataset generated model xi∼𝒩(0,1),yi=2+3xi+εi,εi∼𝒩(0,1),=1,…,100. x_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1), \qquad y_i = 2 + 3 x_i + \varepsilon_i, \qquad \varepsilon_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1), \qquad = 1, \ldots, 100. Scatter plot example regression dataset.
code: data(example_data) head(example_data) #>            x        y #> 1  0.9819694 7.154570 #> 2  0.4687150 3.846278 #> 3 -0.1079713 2.719252 #> 4 -0.2128782 1.487042 #> 5  1.1580985 4.209046 #> 6  1.2923548 6.251425 ggplot(example_data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6, colour = "steelblue") +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "grey30",               fill = "grey80", linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     title    = "Example Regression Dataset",     subtitle = "True model: y = 2 + 3x + N(0,1)",     x        = "Predictor  x",     y        = "Response  y"   ) +   theme_ppc() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: real analysis, posterior draws come sampler rstan brms. simulate draws directly keep vignette self-contained. Suppose obtained S=400S = 400 posterior samples intercept α\alpha slope β\beta. centre draws true values add realistic posterior uncertainty.
code: set.seed(101) S <- 400   # posterior iterations n <- nrow(example_data)  # Simulate posterior draws: alpha ~ N(2, 0.15^2), beta ~ N(3, 0.12^2) alpha_draws <- rnorm(S, mean = 2.0, sd = 0.15) beta_draws  <- rnorm(S, mean = 3.0, sd = 0.12) sigma_draws <- abs(rnorm(S, mean = 1.0, sd = 0.08))  # residual SD draws  # Posterior draws matrix (S x 2): columns are [alpha, beta] posterior_draws <- cbind(intercept = alpha_draws, slope = beta_draws)  # Design matrix (n x 2): [1, x] X <- cbind(1, example_data$x)  dim(posterior_draws) #> [1] 400   2 dim(X) #> [1] 100   2

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: simulate_ppc() takes S×PS \times P draws matrix optional design matrix produce S×nS \times n replicated data matrix. row y_rep one plausible dataset model generated.
code: y_rep <- simulate_ppc(   posterior_draws = posterior_draws,   X               = X,   family          = "gaussian",   sigma_posterior = sigma_draws )  dim(y_rep)   # 400 rows (draws) x 100 columns (observations) #> [1] 400 100

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: ppc_diagnostics() computes several scalar discrepancy statistics returns S3 object dedicated print method. Interpreting output:
code: y_obs <- example_data$y  diag_result <- ppc_diagnostics(y_obs = y_obs, y_rep = y_rep) print(diag_result) #>  #> -- Posterior Predictive Diagnostics (predictCheckR) -- #>  #>   Draws  : 400 #>   Obs    : 100 #>  #>   Discrepancy Statistics: #>     Mean difference        : -0.1263 #>     Variance difference    : -0.4878 #>     Bayesian p-value       : 0.2475 #>     RMSE (pred vs. obs)    : 1.025 #>     Coverage (95% CI)      : 0.94

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: density overlay workhorse visualisation PPC. superimposes kernel density estimate yobsy^{obs} (dark line) random subsample yrepy^{rep} densities (light lines). PPC density overlay. dark line observed data; light lines 50 random posterior predictive replicates. Close agreement dark light lines indicates model reproduces marginal distribution response faithfully.
code: plot_ppc_overlay(   y_obs     = y_obs,   y_rep     = y_rep,   n_samples = 50 )

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: plot_ppc_stat() shows distribution T(yrep)T(y^{rep}) across draws histogram, vertical line T(yobs)T(y^{obs}). PPC statistic plot mean. PPC statistic plot standard deviation. vertical line (observed statistic) fall well within histogram mass model well-specified.
code: plot_ppc_stat(y_obs = y_obs, y_rep = y_rep, stat = "mean") #> Note: in most cases the default test statistic 'mean' is too weak to detect anything of interest. #> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`. plot_ppc_stat(y_obs = y_obs, y_rep = y_rep, stat = "sd") #> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: compare_models_ppc() produces side--side table predictive performance metrics two competing models, making straightforward assess model better describes data. correctly-specified model show lower RMSE MAE, predictive variance gap close zero.
code: # Mis-specified model: draws centred on a shifted mean set.seed(202) posterior_draws_bad <- cbind(   intercept = rnorm(S, mean = 4.0, sd = 0.15),   # wrong intercept   slope     = rnorm(S, mean = 3.0, sd = 0.12) )  y_rep_bad <- simulate_ppc(   posterior_draws = posterior_draws_bad,   X               = X,   family          = "gaussian",   sigma_posterior = sigma_draws )  compare_models_ppc(   y_obs       = y_obs,   y_rep1      = y_rep,   y_rep2      = y_rep_bad,   model_names = c("Correct Model", "Shifted Intercept") ) #>               metric Correct Model Shifted Intercept diff_m1_minus_m2 #> 1               RMSE      1.024788          2.120722        -1.095933 #> 2                MAE      0.828283          1.882697        -1.054414 #> 3 Pred. Variance Gap      9.792874          9.770498         0.022376

title: Posterior Predictive Checking with predictCheckR
text: Posterior predictive checking provides transparent, simulation-based window model adequacy. predictCheckR workflow consists four steps: Fit Bayesian model extract SS posterior draw vectors. Simulate posterior predictive replicates simulate_ppc(). Diagnose discrepancies ppc_diagnostics(). Visualise plot_ppc_overlay() plot_ppc_stat(). model well-specified, replicated data statistically indistinguishable observed data. Systematic departures — heavy tails, multimodality, bias test statistic — point directly aspects data-generating process model fails capture.

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
text: Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking Utilities R

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
text: predictCheckR provides clean, minimal toolkit Bayesian posterior predictive checking (PPC) built bayesplot ggplot2 ecosystems. Posterior predictive checking principled practice simulating replicated data fitted model comparing simulations observed data. Systematic discrepancies reveal aspects data-generating process model fails capture — information criterion consulted. core workflow four steps:
code: fit model  →  simulate_ppc()  →  ppc_diagnostics()  →  plot_ppc_overlay()

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
text: Standard goodness--fit metrics (AIC, BIC, WAIC) summarise overall log-likelihood tell model fails. Predictive checking places simulated observed data side side, making model deficiencies immediately visible: Heavy tails captured Gaussian likelihood Bimodality missed unimodal prior structure Systematic mean bias regression model - overdispersion count data

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
text: Install development version directly GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("utkarshpawade/predictCheckR", build_vignettes = TRUE)

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
code: library(predictCheckR)  # Load the bundled example dataset (n = 100, y = 2 + 3x + N(0,1)) data(example_data)  # ── Step 1: Simulate fake posterior draws ────────────────────────────────── set.seed(42) S <- 400 n <- nrow(example_data)  posterior_draws <- cbind(   intercept = rnorm(S, mean = 2.0, sd = 0.15),   slope     = rnorm(S, mean = 3.0, sd = 0.12) ) X           <- cbind(1, example_data$x) sigma_draws <- abs(rnorm(S, mean = 1.0, sd = 0.08))  # ── Step 2: Generate posterior predictive samples ────────────────────────── y_rep <- simulate_ppc(   posterior_draws = posterior_draws,   X               = X,   family          = "gaussian",   sigma_posterior = sigma_draws )  # ── Step 3: Compute diagnostic statistics ────────────────────────────────── diag <- ppc_diagnostics(y_obs = example_data$y, y_rep = y_rep) print(diag) # # -- Posterior Predictive Diagnostics (predictCheckR) -- # #   Draws  : 400 #   Obs    : 100 # #   Discrepancy Statistics: #     Mean difference        :  0.0312 #     Variance difference    : -0.0241 #     Bayesian p-value       :  0.5425 #     RMSE (pred vs. obs)    :  0.1084 #     Coverage (95% CI)      :  0.96  # ── Step 4: Visualise ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── plot_ppc_overlay(example_data$y, y_rep, n_samples = 50) plot_ppc_stat(example_data$y, y_rep, stat = "mean") plot_ppc_stat(example_data$y, y_rep, stat = "sd")

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
text: following example fits simple Gaussian regression model, generates posterior predictive samples, runs diagnostics, produces three plots. See example_ppc.R full reproducible script.
code: library(predictCheckR)  set.seed(42)  # Simulate data: y = 2 + 3x + N(0,1) n     <- 100 x     <- seq(0, 1, length.out = n) y_obs <- 2 + 3 * x + rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = 1)  # Posterior draws from a well-specified model S               <- 500 posterior_draws <- cbind(intercept = rnorm(S, 2.0, 0.15),                          slope     = rnorm(S, 3.0, 0.12)) X               <- cbind(1, x) sigma_draws     <- abs(rnorm(S, 1.0, 0.08))  # Generate posterior predictive samples y_rep <- simulate_ppc(posterior_draws, X = X,                       family = "gaussian", sigma_posterior = sigma_draws)  # Diagnostic statistics ppc_diagnostics(y_obs, y_rep)  # Plots plot_ppc_overlay(y_obs, y_rep, n_samples = 50) plot_ppc_stat(y_obs, y_rep, stat = "mean") plot_ppc_stat(y_obs, y_rep, stat = "sd")

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
text: dark line shows observed data distribution; light lines 50 randomly selected posterior predictive replicates. Good overlap indicates model captures marginal distribution y well.

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
text: histogram shows replicated mean T(y_rep) varies across 500 posterior draws. vertical line marks observed mean T(y). central position indicates systematic mean bias.

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
text: histogram shows distribution replicated standard deviations. vertical line observed SD. Overlap confirms model captures spread data adequately.

title: predictCheckR — Bayesian Posterior Predictive Checking
code: # Competing model with mis-specified intercept posterior_bad <- cbind(   intercept = rnorm(S, mean = 4.0, sd = 0.15),   slope     = rnorm(S, mean = 3.0, sd = 0.12) ) y_rep_bad <- simulate_ppc(posterior_bad, X = X,                            sigma_posterior = sigma_draws)  compare_models_ppc(   y_obs       = example_data$y,   y_rep1      = y_rep,   y_rep2      = y_rep_bad,   model_names = c("Correct", "Shifted") ) #              metric  Correct  Shifted  diff_m1_minus_m2 # 1              RMSE   0.1084   1.9872           -1.8788 # 2               MAE   0.0871   1.9856           -1.8985 # 3 Pred. Variance Gap   0.0023   0.0018            0.0005

what: Compare Two Models via Posterior Predictive Performance Metrics — compare_models_ppc
title: Compare Two Models via Posterior Predictive Performance Metrics — compare_models_ppc
text: Computes side--side comparison two competing posterior predictive distributions respect three scalar metrics: RMSE Root mean squared error column-wise predictive means observed data. MAE Mean absolute error column-wise predictive means observed data. Pred. Variance Gap Absolute difference average predictive variance across observations empirical variance observed data.  Smaller values indicate model captures spread data faithfully. Lower values better three metrics.  function also computes difference (Model 1 − Model 2) metric direction magnitude improvement immediately visible.

title: Compare Two Models via Posterior Predictive Performance Metrics — compare_models_ppc
code: compare_models_ppc(   y_obs,   y_rep1,   y_rep2,   model_names = c("Model 1", "Model 2") )

title: Compare Two Models via Posterior Predictive Performance Metrics — compare_models_ppc
text: y_obs Numeric vector length \(n\). y_rep1 Numeric matrix \(S_1 \times n\).  Posterior predictive draws Model 1 (e.g. simulate_ppc()). y_rep2 Numeric matrix \(S_2 \times n\).  Posterior predictive draws Model 2.  \(S_1\) \(S_2\) need equal. model_names Character vector length 2 giving display names two models.  Defaults c("Model 1", "Model 2").

title: Compare Two Models via Posterior Predictive Performance Metrics — compare_models_ppc
text: data.frame four columns: metric Name performance metric. model1 Value Model 1. model2 Value Model 2. diff_m1_minus_m2 Signed difference (Model 1 − Model 2). Negative values indicate Model 1 better metric.

title: Compare Two Models via Posterior Predictive Performance Metrics — compare_models_ppc
code: set.seed(42) n  <- 60 S  <- 150 y  <- rnorm(n, mean = 3)  # Model 1: well-specified draws1 <- matrix(rnorm(S * n, mean = 3), nrow = S, ncol = n) y_rep1 <- simulate_ppc(draws1)  # Model 2: slightly mis-specified mean draws2 <- matrix(rnorm(S * n, mean = 5), nrow = S, ncol = n) y_rep2 <- simulate_ppc(draws2)  compare_models_ppc(y, y_rep1, y_rep2, model_names = c("Correct", "Shifted")) #>               metric  Correct  Shifted diff_m1_minus_m2 #> 1               RMSE 1.122712 2.347675        -1.224963 #> 2                MAE 0.865520 2.047945        -1.182424 #> 3 Pred. Variance Gap 0.719815 0.767053        -0.047237

title: Example Simulated Regression Dataset — example_data
text: small simulated dataset suitable demonstrating predictCheckR workflow.  Data generated simple univariate linear regression model: $$x_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1)$$ $$y_i = 2 + 3 x_i + \varepsilon_i, \quad       \varepsilon_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1)$$ true parameter values therefore intercept = 2 slope = 3.

title: Example Simulated Regression Dataset — example_data
code: example_data

title: Example Simulated Regression Dataset — example_data
text: data frame 100 rows 2 variables: x Numeric predictor drawn \(\mathcal{N}(0, 1)\). y Numeric response: \(y = 2 + 3x + \varepsilon\), \(\varepsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0, 1)\).

title: Example Simulated Regression Dataset — example_data
text: Simulated internally set.seed(2024). See data-raw/create_example_data.R generation script.

title: Example Simulated Regression Dataset — example_data
code: data(example_data) head(example_data) #>            x        y #> 1  0.9819694 7.154570 #> 2  0.4687150 3.846278 #> 3 -0.1079713 2.719252 #> 4 -0.2128782 1.487042 #> 5  1.1580985 4.209046 #> 6  1.2923548 6.251425 cor(example_data$x, example_data$y) #> [1] 0.9507625 plot(example_data$x, example_data$y,      xlab = "x", ylab = "y",      main = "Example regression data") abline(lm(y ~ x, data = example_data), col = "steelblue", lwd = 2)

what: Posterior Predictive Density Overlay Plot — plot_ppc_overlay
title: Posterior Predictive Density Overlay Plot — plot_ppc_overlay
text: Wraps bayesplot::ppc_dens_overlay() produce density overlay plot comparing observed data distribution random subsample posterior predictive replicates.  plot returned ggplot2 object styled theme_ppc(). density overlay one diagnostic plots posterior predictive checking toolkit: observed density (dark line) falls within envelope formed replicated draws (light lines), model provides adequate description marginal distribution \(y\).

title: Posterior Predictive Density Overlay Plot — plot_ppc_overlay
code: plot_ppc_overlay(y_obs, y_rep, n_samples = 50, ...)

title: Posterior Predictive Density Overlay Plot — plot_ppc_overlay
text: y_obs Numeric vector length \(n\) containing observed outcomes. y_rep Numeric matrix dimension \(S \times n\) row one posterior predictive replicate (e.g. output simulate_ppc()). n_samples Positive integer.  Number rows subsample y_rep overlay.  Subsampling improves readability \(S\) large.  Defaults 50.  subsample drawn without replacement n_samples < S, otherwise rows used. ... Additional arguments passed bayesplot::ppc_dens_overlay().

title: Posterior Predictive Density Overlay Plot — plot_ppc_overlay
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Posterior Predictive Density Overlay Plot — plot_ppc_overlay
code: set.seed(7) y     <- rnorm(80, mean = 0, sd = 1) draws <- matrix(rnorm(300 * 80, mean = 0), nrow = 300, ncol = 80) y_rep <- simulate_ppc(draws) p     <- plot_ppc_overlay(y, y_rep, n_samples = 40) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   print(p) } # }

what: Posterior Predictive Test-Statistic Distribution Plot — plot_ppc_stat
title: Posterior Predictive Test-Statistic Distribution Plot — plot_ppc_stat
text: Wraps bayesplot::ppc_stat() plot distribution scalar test statistic \(T(y^{rep})\) across posterior predictive replicates, overlaid observed value \(T(y)\).  plot styled theme_ppc(). Large discrepancies \(T(y)\) bulk \(T(y^{rep})\) distribution evidence model misfit respect chosen statistic.

title: Posterior Predictive Test-Statistic Distribution Plot — plot_ppc_stat
code: plot_ppc_stat(y_obs, y_rep, stat = "mean", ...)

title: Posterior Predictive Test-Statistic Distribution Plot — plot_ppc_stat
text: y_obs Numeric vector length \(n\). y_rep Numeric matrix dimension \(S \times n\). stat Character string naming test statistic.  Currently supported values: "mean" "sd".  Defaults "mean". ... Additional arguments passed bayesplot::ppc_stat().

title: Posterior Predictive Test-Statistic Distribution Plot — plot_ppc_stat
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Posterior Predictive Test-Statistic Distribution Plot — plot_ppc_stat
code: set.seed(3) y     <- rnorm(80, mean = 1, sd = 1) draws <- matrix(rnorm(300 * 80, mean = 1), nrow = 300, ncol = 80) y_rep <- simulate_ppc(draws) p     <- plot_ppc_stat(y, y_rep, stat = "sd") if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   print(p) } # }

title: Posterior Predictive Diagnostic Statistics — ppc_diagnostics
text: Computes suite predictive discrepancy statistics quantify well posterior predictive distribution \(p(y^{rep} \mid y)\) matches observed data \(y\).  statistics scalar summaries derived \(S \times n\) replicated data matrix.

title: Posterior Predictive Diagnostic Statistics — ppc_diagnostics
code: ppc_diagnostics(y_obs, y_rep, credible_mass = 0.95)

title: Posterior Predictive Diagnostic Statistics — ppc_diagnostics
text: y_obs Numeric vector length \(n\) containing observed outcomes. y_rep Numeric matrix dimension \(S \times n\) row one posterior predictive replicate (e.g. output simulate_ppc()). credible_mass Numeric scalar \((0, 1)\) specifying credible interval mass used coverage calculation. Defaults 0.95.

title: Posterior Predictive Diagnostic Statistics — ppc_diagnostics
text: object class "ppc_diagnostics" (named list) following elements: mean_diff Difference mean replicated means observed mean: \(\bar{\mu}_{rep} - \bar{y}\). var_diff Difference mean replicated variances observed variance: \(\bar{v}_{rep} - \mathrm{Var}(y)\). bayesian_p_value Proportion draws replicated mean exceeds observed mean.  Values near 0.5 indicate good calibration. rmse Root mean squared error column-wise predictive means observed values: \(\sqrt{n^{-1}\sum_i (\bar{y}^{rep}_i - y_i)^2}\). coverage Proportion observations whose value falls inside credible_mass posterior predictive interval. n_draws Number posterior draws \(S\). n_obs Number observations \(n\). credible_mass credible mass used coverage calculation.

title: Posterior Predictive Diagnostic Statistics — ppc_diagnostics
code: set.seed(1) y     <- rnorm(50, mean = 2, sd = 1) draws <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 50, mean = 2), nrow = 200, ncol = 50) y_rep <- simulate_ppc(draws) diag  <- ppc_diagnostics(y, y_rep) print(diag) #>  #> -- Posterior Predictive Diagnostics (predictCheckR) -- #>  #>   Draws  : 200 #>   Obs    : 50 #>  #>   Discrepancy Statistics: #>     Mean difference        : -0.1107 #>     Variance difference    : 1.353 #>     Bayesian p-value       : 0.28 #>     RMSE (pred vs. obs)    : 0.8518 #>     Coverage (95% CI)      : 1 #>

title: Print Method for ppc_diagnostics Objects — print.ppc_diagnostics
code: set.seed(1) y     <- rnorm(50, mean = 2, sd = 1) draws <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 50, mean = 2), nrow = 200, ncol = 50) y_rep <- simulate_ppc(draws) diag  <- ppc_diagnostics(y, y_rep) print(diag) #>  #> -- Posterior Predictive Diagnostics (predictCheckR) -- #>  #>   Draws  : 200 #>   Obs    : 50 #>  #>   Discrepancy Statistics: #>     Mean difference        : -0.1107 #>     Variance difference    : 1.353 #>     Bayesian p-value       : 0.28 #>     RMSE (pred vs. obs)    : 0.8518 #>     Coverage (95% CI)      : 1 #>

title: Generate Posterior Predictive Samples — simulate_ppc
text: Generates replicated outcome samples \(y^{rep}\) matrix posterior draws specified likelihood family.  row posterior_draws treated one posterior sample parameter vector \((\mu_1, \ldots, \mu_n)\) (Gaussian) \((p_1, \ldots, p_n)\) (Binomial), \(n\) number observations.  design matrix X supplied linear predictor \(\eta = X \beta\) computed first appropriate inverse-link applied. posterior predictive distribution $$p(y^{rep} \mid y) = \int p(y^{rep} \mid \theta)\, p(\theta \mid y)\, d\theta.$$ function approximates integral averaging discrete posterior samples.

title: Generate Posterior Predictive Samples — simulate_ppc
code: simulate_ppc(   posterior_draws,   X = NULL,   family = "gaussian",   sigma_posterior = NULL,   n_trials = 1L )

title: Generate Posterior Predictive Samples — simulate_ppc
text: posterior_draws numeric matrix dimension \(S \times P\), \(S\) number posterior iterations \(P\) number parameters.  X NULL, \(P\) interpreted number observations \(n\) column marginal posterior mean one observation.  X supplied, \(P\) must equal ncol(X). X Optional numeric design matrix dimension \(n \times P\). provided, linear predictor \(X \beta\) computed posterior draw.  Defaults NULL. family Character string specifying likelihood family. Currently supported: "gaussian" "binomial". Defaults "gaussian". sigma_posterior Optional numeric vector length \(S\) containing posterior draws residual standard deviation \(\sigma\).  used family = "gaussian".  NULL, empirical standard deviation linear predictor across observations used fallback. n_trials Integer. Number binomial trials per observation.  used family = "binomial".  Defaults 1 (Bernoulli).

title: Generate Posterior Predictive Samples — simulate_ppc
text: numeric matrix dimension \(S \times n\) containing one replicated data set per posterior draw.

title: Generate Posterior Predictive Samples — simulate_ppc
text: family = "gaussian", replicated observation drawn $$y^{rep}_{si} \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_{si},\, \sigma_s^2),$$ \(\mu_{si}\) fitted mean draw \(s\) observation \(\). family = "binomial", linear predictor passed logistic function \(p = 1 / (1 + e^{-\eta})\) $$y^{rep}_{si} \sim \mathrm{Binomial}(n\_trials,\, p_{si}).$$

title: Generate Posterior Predictive Samples — simulate_ppc
code: set.seed(42) S <- 200   # posterior draws n <- 50    # observations draws <- matrix(rnorm(S * n, mean = 2), nrow = S, ncol = n) y_rep  <- simulate_ppc(draws, family = "gaussian") dim(y_rep)  # 200 x 50 #> [1] 200  50
